content: SaaS microcopy conventions: plain language, concise, user-goal-first over brand voice. Buttons = verb + object ('Download report', 'Create deal'), tell users exactly what happens. Error messages guide to resolution, never assign blame; specific errors with resolution steps reduce form abandonment up to ~40%. Helper text explains unusual requirements and shows format examples inline (e.g. date format, password rules). Consistent terminology and tone across the product; users scan, so short sentences, no fluff. Sentence case is the dominant modern SaaS convention for UI labels (Material, most design systems) though sources emphasize consistency over the specific choice.
